# 2.10.4.9.2 — Bubble overlay → backend sync (DONE)

**Status:** **DONE** — daemon §1–§6 + V2 client §7–§10 in tree; user verified. **⏳** Meson flip (V2 / `FileVerification` not linked) — tracked in [`2.10.4.9-ACTIVE-v2-caller-cutover.md`](../2.10.4.9-ACTIVE-v2-caller-cutover.md)

**Parent:** [`2.10.4.9-ACTIVE-v2-caller-cutover.md`](../2.10.4.9-ACTIVE-v2-caller-cutover.md)

**Pointer:** `docs/guide-to-writing-plans.md`

**Scope note (🔷):** Client/in-process hunks target **`libocfiles/V2/`** and **`liboctools/*V2/`** only — **🚫** do not edit shipping **`libocfiles/`** (frozen until cutover flip).

---

## Implementation progress

| § | Path | ✔️ / ⏳ | Notes |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 1 | `ollmfilesd/File.vala` | **✔️** | `to_real`, `realize` |
| 2 | `ollmfilesd/ProjectManager.vala` | **✔️** | `convert_fake_file_to_real` deleted |
| 3 | `ollmfilesd/File.vala` | **✔️** | `rpc_register` → `fake.to_real` |
| 4 | `ollmfilesd/Folder.vala` | **✔️** | `to_real`, `realize` |
| 5 | `ollmfilesd/FileAlias.vala` | **✔️** | `to_real`, `realize` |
| 6 | `ollmfilesd/File.vala` | **✔️** | thin `rpc_write` + private `write` |
| 7 | `liboctools/FileVerification.vala` | **✔️** code · **⏳** Meson | one `File.write` RPC per event; not in `liboctools/meson.build` yet |
| 8 | `libocfiles/V2/File.vala` | **✔️** | client `to_real()` → `register` RPC (not daemon body) |
| 9 | `libocfiles/V2/ProjectManager.vala` | **✔️** N/A | never had `convert_fake_file_to_real` |
| 10a | `liboctools/WriteFileV2/Request.vala` | **✔️** | `yield file.to_real()` |
| 10b | `liboctools/EditModeV2/Stream.vala` | **✔️** | `yield file.to_real()`; removed `convert_new_file_to_real` |

**Build:** `ollmfilesd` compiles with §1–§6. V2 / `*V2` / `FileVerification` exist in tree but are **⏳** until [`2.10.4.9`](../2.10.4.9-ACTIVE-v2-caller-cutover.md) Meson flip.

**Frozen (unchanged, still old pattern — not in this plan):** shipping `libocfiles/File.vala`, `libocfiles/ProjectManager.vala`, `liboctools/WriteFile/Request.vala`, `liboctools/EditMode/Stream.vala`.

---

## Purpose

- **🔷** Bubble overlay apply: sync new/changed paths from bubble upper layer onto the real project tree and the files daemon index.
- **🔷** `FileVerification` sends one **`File.write`** RPC per scan event (no separate register from the overlay client).

---

## Agreed (user)

- **🔷** Scan is recursive; parent dirs get **`created`** before children in the same pass.
- **🔷** On the backend, **`File.write`** runs **`to_real`** (DB/index) when needed, then **`realize`** on disk — owned by the **`File` / `Folder` / `FileAlias` object**, not the RPC relay.
- **🔷** New method name: **`realize`** — apply {@link FileParams} on disk (write / mkdir / symlink + chmod). Index row must exist first ({@code id >= 0}); call **`to_real`** when still fake.
- **🔷** **`to_real`** is index/DB only — no filesystem I/O.
- **🚫** no **`write_from_params`** (or similar) — disk apply is **`realize`** only; new vs existing differs only in whether **`to_real`** runs first.
- **🚫** no new **`ensure_indexed`** (or similar) helper — fake → real uses **existing** codebase paths (`to_real`, `saveToDB`, `make_children`, …), refactored onto the object, not a new abstraction.
- **🚫** no **`ProjectManager.convert_fake_file_to_real`** delegate — delete from PM; callers use **`yield file.to_real()`** (or **`Folder` / `FileAlias`** equivalent).

---

## Context

- **ℹ️** Scan walk: `libocbwrap/Scan.vala` — `scan_dir` handles entries at one level, recurses into subdirs after **`handle_folder`**.
- **ℹ️** Client reads **`overlay_path`** locally; RPC uses **`real_path`** + `FileParams` (`path`, `content`, `base_type`, `target`, `unix_mode`).

---

## Current tree

- **✔️** **`realize`** on daemon `File` / `Folder` / `FileAlias` (`ollmfilesd/`)
- **✔️** fake → real on daemon: **`File.to_real`**; PM relay deleted (§2)
- **✔️** **`rpc_write`**: private `write` + object `to_real` / `realize` (§6)
- **✔️** **`FileVerification`**: one `File.write(content, base_type, target, unix_mode)` per event (§7)
- **✔️** V2 client **`File.to_real`** + caller cutover in `WriteFileV2` / `EditModeV2` (§8–§10)
- **⏳** Meson: V2 client API + `FileVerification` not in build lists yet
- **🚫** shipping `libocfiles` / `WriteFile` / `EditMode` — frozen until flip (**🚫** not in this plan)

---

## Concrete code proposals

Intro: hunks are **Remove** / **Replace with** / **Add** from the tree; apply **§1 → §10** in order. Status in each **`###`** heading: **✔️** agent done · **⏳** backlog.

Proposal fences follow **`temporary-variables`**: locals use **`var`** only (no `FileBase?`, `string`, … on locals); no trivial property aliases (`var x = this.manager.active_project`, `var path = GLib.Path.get_dirname(…)` when used once); long chains (4+ steps) may alias.

---

### ✔️ 1. `ollmfilesd/File.vala` — `to_real`, `realize`

**Why:** Move fake → real off `ProjectManager` onto `File`; **`realize`** applies overlay params on disk (new or existing indexed file).

**Where:** `File` class, before the closing `}` of the class (after `revert` method).

**Depends on:** none.

#### Add — index + FS helpers on `File`, after `revert` method

```vala
		/**
		 * Promote fake file ({@code id == -1}) to indexed row (DB + parent chain).
		 * Does not touch the filesystem — call {@link realize} after.
		 */
		public async void to_real() throws Error
		{
			if (this.id != -1) {
				return;
			}
			if (this.manager.active_project == null) {
				return;
			}
			var found_base_folder = this.manager.active_project.project_files.find_container_of(
				GLib.Path.get_dirname(this.path)
			);
			if (found_base_folder == null) {
				return;
			}
			var parent_folder = yield found_base_folder.make_children(this.path);
			if (parent_folder == null) {
				throw new GLib.IOError.FAILED(
					"Could not create parent folder for "
					+ this.path
				);
			}
			this.parent = parent_folder;
			this.parent_id = parent_folder.id;
			this.id = 0;
			this.detect_language();
			if (this.language != "") {
				this.is_text = true;
			}
			parent_folder.children.append(this);
			this.manager.buffer_provider.create_buffer(this);
			if (this.manager.db != null) {
				this.saveToDB(this.manager.db, null, false);
			}
			if (this.manager.db == null) {
				this.manager.file_cache.set(this.path, this);
			}
			this.manager.active_project.project_files.update_from(
				this.manager.active_project
			);
			this.manager.active_project.project_files.new_file_added(this);
		}

		/**
		 * Apply {@link FileParams} on disk for an indexed file (write bytes + mode).
		 */
		public async void realize(FileParams p) throws Error
		{
			if (this.buffer == null) {
				this.manager.buffer_provider.create_buffer(this);
			}
			yield this.buffer.write_real(p.content);
			if (p.unix_mode == 0) {
				return;
			}
			if (Posix.chmod(
				this.path,
				(Posix.mode_t) (p.unix_mode & 0777)
			) != 0) {
				throw new GLib.IOError.FAILED(GLib.strerror(Posix.errno));
			}
		}
```

---

### ✔️ 2. `ollmfilesd/ProjectManager.vala` — delete `convert_fake_file_to_real`

**Why:** Logic moves to `File.to_real`; **🚫** no PM relay.

**Where:** `ProjectManager.convert_fake_file_to_real` method (docblock + body).

**Depends on:** §1.

#### Remove

```vala
		/**
		 * Converts a fake file (id = -1) to a real File object if it's within the active project.
		 * 
		 * This method:
		 * - Checks if the file is within the active project
		 * - Finds or creates parent folder objects in the project tree
		 * - Queries file info from disk
		 * - Converts the fake file to a real File object
		 * - Saves the file to the database
		 * - Updates the ProjectFiles list
		 * - Emits the new_file_added signal
		 * 
		 * @param file The fake file to convert (must have id = -1)
		 * @param file_path The normalized file path
		 */
		public async void convert_fake_file_to_real(File file, string file_path) throws Error
		{
			var active_project = this.active_project;
			
			// Early return if no active project
			if (active_project == null) {
				// File is outside project - keep as fake file (id = -1)
				return;
			}
			
			// Get parent directory path
			// We may have created multiple subdirectories when creating the file,
			// so use find_container_of to find the closest existing folder in the project
			var parent_dir_path = GLib.Path.get_dirname(file_path);
			var found_base_folder = active_project.project_files.find_container_of(parent_dir_path);
			
			// If we didn't find any parent folder in the project, the file is outside the project
			if (found_base_folder == null) {
				// File is outside project - keep as fake file (id = -1)
				return;
			}
			
			// Create missing child folders from found_base_folder down to parent_dir_path
			var parent_folder = yield found_base_folder.make_children(file_path);
			if (parent_folder == null) {
				GLib.warning("ProjectManager.convert_fake_file_to_real: Could not create parent folder for %s", file_path);
				return;
			}
			
			// Query file info from disk
			var gfile = GLib.File.new_for_path(file_path);
			if (!gfile.query_exists()) {
				GLib.warning("ProjectManager.convert_fake_file_to_real: File does not exist on disk: %s", file_path);
				return;
			}
			
			GLib.FileInfo file_info;
			try {
				file_info = gfile.query_info(
					GLib.FileAttribute.STANDARD_CONTENT_TYPE + "," + GLib.FileAttribute.TIME_MODIFIED,
					GLib.FileQueryInfoFlags.NONE,
					null
				);
			} catch (GLib.Error e) {
				GLib.warning("ProjectManager.convert_fake_file_to_real: Could not query file info for %s: %s", file_path, e.message);
				return;
			}
			
			// Convert fake file to real File object
			// Create new File (not new_fake)
			var real_file = new File(this) {
				path = file_path,
				parent = parent_folder,
				parent_id = parent_folder.id,
				id = 0 // New file, will be inserted on save
			};
			
			// Set properties from FileInfo
			var content_type = file_info.get_content_type();
			real_file.is_text = content_type != null && content_type != "" && content_type.has_prefix("text/");
			
			var mod_time = file_info.get_modification_date_time();
			if (mod_time != null) {
				real_file.last_modified = mod_time.to_unix();
			}
			
			// Detect language from filename using buffer provider
			var detected_language = this.buffer_provider.detect_language(real_file);
			if (detected_language != "") {
				real_file.language = detected_language;
			}
			if (!real_file.is_text && real_file.language != "") {
				real_file.is_text = true;
			}
			
			// Add file to parent folder's children
			parent_folder.children.append(real_file);
			
			// Create buffer for new file object
			// The old buffer was associated with the fake file, so we create a new one
			// The file was just written to disk, so the buffer will read from disk when needed
			this.buffer_provider.create_buffer(real_file);
			
			// Save file to DB (gets id > 0, and adds to file_cache automatically)
			if (this.db != null) {
				real_file.saveToDB(this.db, null, false);
			} else {
				// If no DB, manually add to file_cache for immediate lookup
				this.file_cache.set(real_file.path, real_file);
			}
			
			// Update ProjectFiles list
			active_project.project_files.update_from(active_project);
			
			// Manually emit new_file_added signal
			active_project.project_files.new_file_added(real_file);
		}
		
```

---

### ✔️ 3. `ollmfilesd/File.vala` — `rpc_register` calls `File.to_real`

**Why:** Replace PM caller; `fake` is indexed in place.

**Where:** `construct` block — `this.rpc_register.connect` handler, fake branch.

**Depends on:** §1, §2.

#### Remove

```vala
				this.manager.convert_fake_file_to_real.begin(
					fake,
					p.path,
					(obj, res) => {
						this.manager.convert_fake_file_to_real.end(res);
						var real = this.manager.get_file_from_active_project(
							p.path
						);
						var row = new File(this.manager);
						row.copy_from(real, {"manager", "buffer", "parent"});
						row.last_modified = real.mtime_on_disk();
						request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
							id = request.id,
							result = row,
							result_type = "File"
						});
					}
				);
```

#### Replace with

```vala
				fake.to_real.begin((obj, res) => {
					try {
						fake.to_real.end(res);
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
							id = request.id,
							error = new OLLMrpc.Error(
								OLLMrpc.RpcErrorCode.INTERNAL_ERROR,
								e.message
							)
						});
						return;
					}
					var row = new File(this.manager);
					row.copy_from(fake, {"manager", "buffer", "parent"});
					row.last_modified = fake.mtime_on_disk();
					request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
						id = request.id,
						result = row,
						result_type = "File"
					});
				});
```

---

### ✔️ 4. `ollmfilesd/Folder.vala` — `to_real`, `realize`

**Why:** New directory overlay paths index via `Folder`, then **`realize`** mkdir (+ chmod) on disk; existing indexed dirs get mode-only apply via the same **`realize`**.

**Where:** `Folder` class, before its closing `}` (end of file).

**Depends on:** none (parallel with §1).

#### Add — overlay folder apply methods at end of `Folder` class

**ℹ️** Daemon index wiring (parent, FileHistory, `saveToDB`) matches prior overlay folder indexing; **`realize`** holds mkdir (if missing) + chmod.

```vala
		/**
		 * Promote fake folder ({@code id == -1}) to indexed row.
		 */
		public async void to_real() throws Error
		{
			if (this.id != -1) {
				return;
			}
			if (this.manager.active_project == null) {
				return;
			}
			var parent_folder = this.manager.active_project.project_files.folder_map.get(
				GLib.Path.get_dirname(this.path)
			);
			if (parent_folder == null) {
				parent_folder = this.manager.active_project;
			}
			this.parent = parent_folder;
			this.parent_id = parent_folder.id;
			this.id = 0;
			var file_history = new FileHistory(
				this.manager.db,
				this,
				"added",
				new GLib.DateTime.now_local()
			);
			yield file_history.commit();
			parent_folder.children.append(this);
			this.manager.active_project.project_files.folder_map.set(
				this.path,
				this
			);
			this.saveToDB(this.manager.db, null, false);
			this.manager.file_cache.set(this.path, this);
			this.manager.active_project.project_files.update_from(
				this.manager.active_project
			);
		}

		/**
		 * Apply {@link FileParams} on disk (mkdir when missing, then mode).
		 */
		public async void realize(FileParams p) throws Error
		{
			if (!GLib.File.new_for_path(this.path).query_exists()) {
				GLib.File.new_for_path(this.path).make_directory(null);
			}
			if (p.unix_mode == 0) {
				return;
			}
			if (Posix.chmod(
				this.path,
				(Posix.mode_t) (p.unix_mode & 0777)
			) != 0) {
				throw new GLib.IOError.FAILED(GLib.strerror(Posix.errno));
			}
		}
```

---

### ✔️ 5. `ollmfilesd/FileAlias.vala` — `to_real`, `realize`

**Why:** New symlink overlay paths index via `FileAlias`, then **`realize`** creates/replaces symlink on disk.

**Where:** `FileAlias` class, before its closing `}`.

**Depends on:** none (parallel with §1).

#### Add — overlay alias apply methods at end of `FileAlias` class

**ℹ️** Daemon index wiring (FileHistory, parent append, `saveToDB`) matches prior overlay alias indexing; **`realize`** holds symlink + chmod. **`to_real`** takes link text from RPC (`p.target`).

```vala
		/**
		 * Promote fake alias ({@code id == -1}) to indexed row.
		 *
		 * @param link_target Symlink text from overlay / RPC params
		 */
		public async void to_real(string link_target) throws Error
		{
			if (this.id != -1) {
				return;
			}
			if (link_target == "") {
				throw new GLib.IOError.INVALID_ARGUMENT("Symlink target is empty");
			}
			if (this.manager.active_project == null) {
				return;
			}
			var parent_folder = this.manager.active_project.project_files.folder_map.get(
				GLib.Path.get_dirname(this.path)
			);
			if (parent_folder == null) {
				parent_folder = this.manager.active_project;
			}
			this.parent = parent_folder;
			this.parent_id = parent_folder.id;
			this.id = 0;
			this.target_path = link_target;
			var file_history = new FileHistory(
				this.manager.db,
				this,
				"added",
				new GLib.DateTime.now_local()
			);
			yield file_history.commit();
			parent_folder.children.append(this);
			this.saveToDB(this.manager.db, null, false);
			this.manager.file_cache.set(this.path, this);
			this.manager.active_project.project_files.update_from(
				this.manager.active_project
			);
		}

		/**
		 * Apply {@link FileParams} on disk (replace symlink + mode).
		 */
		public async void realize(FileParams p) throws Error
		{
			if (p.target == "") {
				throw new GLib.IOError.INVALID_ARGUMENT("Symlink target is empty");
			}
			if (GLib.FileUtils.test(this.path, GLib.FileTest.EXISTS)) {
				GLib.FileUtils.unlink(this.path);
			}
			GLib.File.new_for_path(this.path).make_symbolic_link(
				p.target,
				null
			);
			if (p.unix_mode == 0) {
				return;
			}
			if (Posix.chmod(
				this.path,
				(Posix.mode_t) (p.unix_mode & 0777)
			) != 0) {
				throw new GLib.IOError.FAILED(GLib.strerror(Posix.errno));
			}
		}
```

---

### ✔️ 6. `ollmfilesd/File.vala` — thin `rpc_write` relay

**Why:** RPC dispatches to object methods only; await before reply.

**Where:** `File` class `construct` block — replace `this.rpc_write.connect` handler; add private async **`write`** after `construct` (same pattern as `Codebase.search` for `rpc_search`).

**Depends on:** §1, §4, §5.

#### Remove

```vala
			this.rpc_write.connect((request) => {
				var p = (FileParams) request.param;
				switch (p.base_type) {
					case "d":
						try {
							GLib.File.new_for_path(p.path).make_directory(null);
						} catch (GLib.Error e) {
							request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
								id = request.id,
								error = new OLLMrpc.Error(
									OLLMrpc.RpcErrorCode.INTERNAL_ERROR,
									e.message
								)
							});
							return;
						}
						break;
					case "fa":
						if (p.target == "") {
							request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
								id = request.id,
								error = new OLLMrpc.Error(
									OLLMrpc.RpcErrorCode.INTERNAL_ERROR,
									"Symlink target is empty"
								)
							});
							return;
						}
						try {
							if (GLib.FileUtils.test(p.path, GLib.FileTest.EXISTS)) {
								GLib.FileUtils.unlink(p.path);
							}
							GLib.File.new_for_path(p.path).make_symbolic_link(
								p.target,
								null
							);
						} catch (GLib.Error e) {
							request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
								id = request.id,
								error = new OLLMrpc.Error(
									OLLMrpc.RpcErrorCode.INTERNAL_ERROR,
									e.message
								)
							});
							return;
						}
						break;
					default:
						var file = this.manager.get_file_from_active_project(p.path);
						if (file == null) {
							file = new File(this.manager) {
								path = p.path,
								id = -1
							};
						}
						if (file.buffer == null) {
							this.manager.buffer_provider.create_buffer(file);
						}
						file.buffer.write_real.begin(p.content);
						break;
				}
				if (p.unix_mode != 0
					&& Posix.chmod(
						p.path,
						(Posix.mode_t) (p.unix_mode & 0777)
					) != 0) {
					request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
						id = request.id,
						error = new OLLMrpc.Error(
							OLLMrpc.RpcErrorCode.INTERNAL_ERROR,
							GLib.strerror(Posix.errno)
						)
					});
					return;
				}
				request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
					msg = "ok"
				});
			});
```

#### Replace with

**No `try`/`catch` in `rpc_write.connect`** — callback is `begin`/`end` only. Error reply lives in **`write`**.

```vala
			this.rpc_write.connect((request) => {
				var p = (FileParams) request.param;
				this.write.begin(
					p,
					request,
					(obj, res) => {
						this.write.end(res);
					}
				);
			});
```

#### Add — private async `write` after `construct` block closes

```vala
		private async void write(
			FileParams p,
			OLLMrpc.Request request
		) {
			try {
				switch (p.base_type) {
					case "d": {
						var folder = this.manager.get_folder_at_path(p.path);
						if (folder == null) {
							folder = new Folder(this.manager) {
								path = p.path,
								id = -1
							};
						}
						if (folder.id < 0) {
							yield folder.to_real();
						}
						yield folder.realize(p);
						break;
					}
					case "fa": {
						var alias = this.manager.file_cache.get(
							p.path
						) as FileAlias;
						if (alias == null) {
							alias = new FileAlias(this.manager) {
								path = p.path,
								id = -1
							};
						}
						if (alias.id < 0) {
							yield alias.to_real(p.target);
						}
						yield alias.realize(p);
						break;
					}
					default: {
						var file = this.manager.get_file_from_active_project(
							p.path
						);
						if (file == null) {
							file = new File(this.manager) {
								path = p.path,
								id = -1
							};
						}
						if (file.id < 0) {
							yield file.to_real();
						}
						yield file.realize(p);
						break;
					}
				}
			} catch (GLib.Error e) {
				request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() {
					id = request.id,
					error = new OLLMrpc.Error(
						OLLMrpc.RpcErrorCode.INTERNAL_ERROR,
						e.message
					)
				});
				return;
			}
			request.reply(new OLLMrpc.Response() { msg = "ok" });
		}
```

---

### ✔️ 7. `liboctools/FileVerification.vala` — one `File.write` per scan event

**Why:** Overlay client sends one RPC; daemon runs index + **`realize`** / update.

**Where:** `created`, `modified`; remove `copy_permissions`.

**Depends on:** §6.

#### Remove

```vala
		public async void created(
			GLib.FileType file_type,
			string real_path,
			string overlay_path)
		{
			if (this.project == null) {
				return;
			}
			switch (file_type) {
				case GLib.FileType.DIRECTORY:
					// Parent dirs are created when child files are written (write_to_disk).
					return;
				case GLib.FileType.SYMBOLIC_LINK:
					// Not a File row — parent read_dir picks up symlinks after disk exists.
					return;
				default:
					var content = "";
					try {
						var bytes = GLib.File.new_for_path(overlay_path).load_bytes(
							null
						);
						content = (string) bytes.get_data();
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot read overlay file (%s): %s",
							overlay_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					var fake = new OLLMfiles.File.new_fake(this.manager, real_path);
					if (!(yield fake.write(content))) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot write new file via RPC (%s)",
							real_path
						);
						return;
					}
					try {
						yield this.project.insert_file(fake, real_path);
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot register new file (%s): %s",
							real_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					yield this.copy_permissions(overlay_path, real_path);
					return;
			}
		}
```

#### Replace with

Full body inlined — same logic in **`created`** and **`modified`** (one `File.write` RPC per event).

```vala
		public async void created(
			GLib.FileType file_type,
			string real_path,
			string overlay_path)
		{
			if (this.project == null) {
				return;
			}
			var base_type = "f";
			var content = "";
			var target = "";
			switch (file_type) {
				case GLib.FileType.DIRECTORY:
					base_type = "d";
					break;
				case GLib.FileType.SYMBOLIC_LINK:
					base_type = "fa";
					try {
						var link = GLib.FileUtils.read_link(overlay_path);
						if (link == null) {
							GLib.warning(
								"Cannot read symlink target (%s)",
								overlay_path
							);
							return;
						}
						target = link;
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot read symlink target (%s): %s",
							overlay_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					break;
				case GLib.FileType.REGULAR:
					try {
						var bytes = GLib.File.new_for_path(
							overlay_path
						).load_bytes(null);
						content = (string) bytes.get_data();
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot read overlay file (%s): %s",
							overlay_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					break;
				default:
					break;
			}
			var unix_mode = 0U;
			try {
				var info = GLib.File.new_for_path(overlay_path).query_info(
					GLib.FileAttribute.UNIX_MODE,
					GLib.FileQueryInfoFlags.NONE,
					null
				);
				unix_mode = info.get_attribute_uint32(
					GLib.FileAttribute.UNIX_MODE
				) & 0777;
			} catch (GLib.Error e) {
				GLib.warning(
					"Cannot query overlay mode (%s): %s",
					overlay_path,
					e.message
				);
			}
			if (!(yield new OLLMfiles.File.new_fake(
				this.manager,
				real_path
			).write(
				content,
				base_type,
				target,
				unix_mode
			))) {
				GLib.warning(
					"Cannot apply overlay write via RPC (%s)",
					real_path
				);
			}
		}
```

#### Remove

```vala
		public async void modified(
			GLib.FileType file_type,
			string real_path,
			string overlay_path)
		{
			if (this.project == null) {
				return;
			}
			switch (file_type) {
				case GLib.FileType.DIRECTORY:
					return;
				case GLib.FileType.SYMBOLIC_LINK:
					// Symlinks are indexed when the parent folder is scanned (read_dir).
					return;
				default:
					var file = yield this.project.fetch_file(real_path);
					if (file == null) {
						return;
					}
					var content = "";
					try {
						var bytes = GLib.File.new_for_path(overlay_path).load_bytes(
							null
						);
						content = (string) bytes.get_data();
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot read overlay file (%s): %s",
							overlay_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					if (!(yield file.write(content))) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot write modified file via RPC (%s)",
							real_path
						);
					}
					yield this.copy_permissions(overlay_path, real_path);
					return;
			}
		}
```

#### Replace with

Same body as **`created`** Replace above.

```vala
		public async void modified(
			GLib.FileType file_type,
			string real_path,
			string overlay_path)
		{
			if (this.project == null) {
				return;
			}
			var base_type = "f";
			var content = "";
			var target = "";
			switch (file_type) {
				case GLib.FileType.DIRECTORY:
					base_type = "d";
					break;
				case GLib.FileType.SYMBOLIC_LINK:
					base_type = "fa";
					try {
						var link = GLib.FileUtils.read_link(overlay_path);
						if (link == null) {
							GLib.warning(
								"Cannot read symlink target (%s)",
								overlay_path
							);
							return;
						}
						target = link;
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot read symlink target (%s): %s",
							overlay_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					break;
				case GLib.FileType.REGULAR:
					try {
						var bytes = GLib.File.new_for_path(
							overlay_path
						).load_bytes(null);
						content = (string) bytes.get_data();
					} catch (GLib.Error e) {
						GLib.warning(
							"Cannot read overlay file (%s): %s",
							overlay_path,
							e.message
						);
						return;
					}
					break;
				default:
					break;
			}
			var unix_mode = 0U;
			try {
				var info = GLib.File.new_for_path(overlay_path).query_info(
					GLib.FileAttribute.UNIX_MODE,
					GLib.FileQueryInfoFlags.NONE,
					null
				);
				unix_mode = info.get_attribute_uint32(
					GLib.FileAttribute.UNIX_MODE
				) & 0777;
			} catch (GLib.Error e) {
				GLib.warning(
					"Cannot query overlay mode (%s): %s",
					overlay_path,
					e.message
				);
			}
			if (!(yield new OLLMfiles.File.new_fake(
				this.manager,
				real_path
			).write(
				content,
				base_type,
				target,
				unix_mode
			))) {
				GLib.warning(
					"Cannot apply overlay write via RPC (%s)",
					real_path
				);
			}
		}
```

#### Remove

```vala
		/**
		 * Apply rwx from bubble overlay to project file via {@code File.apply_permissions}.
		 *
		 * @param overlay_path Overlay file path (bubble sandbox)
		 * @param real_path Real project file path
		 */
		private async void copy_permissions(
			string overlay_path,
			string real_path
		) {
			GLib.FileInfo overlay_info;
			try {
				overlay_info = GLib.File.new_for_path(overlay_path).query_info(
					GLib.FileAttribute.UNIX_MODE,
					GLib.FileQueryInfoFlags.NONE,
					null
				);
			} catch (GLib.Error e) {
				GLib.warning(
					"Cannot query overlay file permissions (%s): %s",
					overlay_path,
					e.message
				);
				return;
			}

			var overlay_rwx = overlay_info.get_attribute_uint32(
				GLib.FileAttribute.UNIX_MODE
			) & 0777;
			var file = new OLLMfiles.File.new_fake(this.manager, real_path);
			if (!(yield file.apply_permissions(overlay_rwx))) {
				GLib.warning(
					"Cannot apply permissions via RPC (%s)",
					real_path
				);
			}
		}
```

**Note:** §7 uses `file.write(content, base_type, target, unix_mode)` from **`libocfiles/V2/File.vala`**. **⏳** add `FileVerification.vala` to **`liboctools/meson.build`** at V2 flip.

---

### ✔️ 8. `libocfiles/V2/File.vala` — client `to_real`

**Why:** V2 thin client — promote fake row via RPC after disk write; **🚫** not shipping `libocfiles/File.vala`.

**Where:** `File` class, after **`register`** method.

**Depends on:** §1 (daemon `File.register` → `fake.to_real`).

**Status:** **✔️** landed — client `to_real()` calls **`register()`** RPC and throws on failure; merges indexed row onto same client instance. **🚫** do not copy daemon §1 body (no local DB on client).

#### Add — client `to_real` (RPC only)

```vala
		/**
		 * Promote fake file ({@code id == -1}) to indexed row on daemon.
		 * Does not write disk bytes — call {@link write} first when needed.
		 */
		public async void to_real() throws Error
		{
			if (this.id != -1) {
				return;
			}
			if (!(yield this.register())) {
				throw new GLib.IOError.FAILED(
					"Could not register file: " + this.path
				);
			}
		}
```

**Also ✔️ in tree:** extended **`write(content, base_type, target, unix_mode)`** and **`apply_permissions`** on V2 `File` (needed by §7).

---

### ✔️ 9. `libocfiles/V2/ProjectManager.vala` — no PM relay

**Why:** V2 PM never had **`convert_fake_file_to_real`** — fake → real is **`File.to_real`** / **`Folder.insert_file`**.

**Status:** **✔️** N/A — nothing to delete. **🚫** shipping **`libocfiles/ProjectManager.vala`** stays frozen until flip.

---

### ✔️ 10. Replace V2 callers (**🚫** not shipping tools)

**Why:** **🚫** no PM relay; callers use **`yield file.to_real()`** after **`File.write`**.

**Depends on:** §8.

**Scope:** **`liboctools/WriteFileV2/`**, **`liboctools/EditModeV2/`** — **🚫** do not edit **`WriteFile/`** or **`EditMode/`** until Meson flip.

#### ✔️ 10a. `liboctools/WriteFileV2/Request.vala` — `execute_request` added-file branch

**Where:** `if (change_type == "added" && this.file.id <= 0 && is_in_project)` block.

#### Remove

```vala
				try {
					yield project_manager.active_project.insert_file(
						this.file,
						this.normalized_path
					);
				} catch (GLib.Error e) {
					GLib.warning(
						"Cannot register new file (%s): %s",
						this.normalized_path,
						e.message
					);
				}
```

#### Replace with

```vala
				try {
					yield this.file.to_real();
				} catch (GLib.Error e) {
					GLib.warning(
						"Cannot register new file (%s): %s",
						this.normalized_path,
						e.message
					);
				}
```

**Note:** V2 has **🚫** no `project_files.update_from` — daemon index is authoritative; client row updated via **`register`** merge inside **`to_real`**.

---

#### ✔️ 10b. `liboctools/EditModeV2/Stream.vala` — `sync_and_update_metadata`

**Where:** added-file branch in **`sync_and_update_metadata`**; delete **`convert_new_file_to_real`** helper.

#### Remove

```vala
				yield this.convert_new_file_to_real(
					this.file.manager,
					this.file
				);
```

#### Replace with

```vala
				yield this.file.to_real();
```

#### Remove — entire helper

```vala
		private async void convert_new_file_to_real(
			OLLMfiles.ProjectManager project_manager,
			OLLMfiles.File file) throws Error
		{
			if (project_manager.active_project == null) {
				return;
			}
			yield project_manager.active_project.insert_file(
				file,
				this.request.normalized_path
			);
		}
```

**Also ✔️:** **`libocfiles/V2/Folder.vala`** **`insert_file`** delegates to **`yield file.to_real()`** (same RPC path as §10a/10b).
